issues.tradition_claim: All composite beings have the three-phase shape: unmanifest before, manifest in the middle, unmanifest after; mourning the middle as if it were the whole is a category error (Shankara: composite-being argument; Ramanuja: epistemic-asymmetry argument).
issues.engineering_claim: Every codebase has the same three-act shape: a customer demand exists before any line is written, a running implementation manifests in production, then a residual specification lives on inside someone's head and within the migration document.
issues.explanation: The engineering layer rescopes from composite living beings to codebases. The three-phase argumentative shape is preserved exactly, including the perceptual-asymmetry move ('the middle act is the only one you can grep for, and it is the smallest of the three'). The project's additive-layer mandate authorizes the domain shift; no weakening or contradiction.
